rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Defendant Pat Reid'S Cross-Complaint By Plaintiff S. Clark, Minor By Jacqueline Reid Under Code Of Civil Procedure 425.16 ("Anti-Slapp" Motion)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 23, 2010, line 4, PLAINTIFF S. CLARK Special Motion to Strike Defendant Pat Reid's Cross-Complaint By Plaintiff S. Clark, Minor By Jacqueline Reid Under Code of Civil Procedure 425.16 ("Anti-Slapp" Motion). Motion denied. The conduct described in the cross-complaint was not protected speech or petitioning activity. Cross-defendant does not specify the specific subsection of CCP section 425.16(e) under which her actions qualify. No evidence supports allegation that cross-defendant's conduct was part of a police investigation or done in serious contemplation of litigation. The conduct did not concern a matter of public interest, but was a private matter. Cross-complainant had not been charged with a crime or even detained by the police. The alleged assault occurred in a private residence between two individuals, while the fliers were posted not only in cross-complainants immediate neighborhood in South Beach, but also in the Mission. Request for attorney fees denied. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SIMMONDS & NARITA, LLP, A LIMITED LIABILITY VS. KENOSIAN & MIELE, LLP A LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Transferring Action And Request For Sanctions Against Pltf'S Counsel; Request For Judicialnotice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 23, 2010, line 10, DEFENDANT KENOSIAN & MIELE, LLP Motion for Order Transferring Action and Request for Sanctions Against Plaintiff's Counsel. Motion for order transferring action to Los Angeles County Superior Court is granted. An action for breach of contract must be tried in the county where the defendant resides or where the contract is to be performed. CCP section 395(a). The contract is silent as to the place of performance, and venue is proper in the county where defendant resides. Request for sanctions denied. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: HOPOLA INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION DBA A BODY OF VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF INDUSTRIAL DIVISION OF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Writ Of Administrative Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, April 23, 2010, line 11, PETITIONER HOPOLA INC. Motion for Writ of Administrative Mandate. Motion denied. Because Labor Code section 3725 & 226.5 require a hearing, the informal hearing provisions of Chapter 4.5 apply. See Corrales vs. Bradstreet (2007) 153 Cal.App. 4th 33. Notice of the informal hearing procedure was provided to the petitioner through the citation to Labor Code section 226.5. Petitioner waived the hearsay objections because notice of the informal nature of the hearing was provided. City of Rancho Cucamonga vs. Regional Water Quality Control Board (2006) 135 Cal.App. 4th 1377. Failure to object to evidence at an administrative hearing operates as a waiver. See Tennant vs. Civil Service Comm. (1946) 77 Cal.App. 2d 489, 498. Petitioner did not object to documents at the hearing and acted as though they were authentic. Petitioner's representative conceded that some of the contract is correct. (RT 26.) Hearsay without objection is sufficient to support a finding in the absence of contrary evidence. Frudden Enters vs. ALRB (1984) 153 Cal.App.3d 262. The hearing officer did not rely solely on hearsay to render her decision. Sullivan made numerous admissions at the hearing, including that she switched her business model from employer/employee to independent contractor. (RT 13-14.) Susbstantial evidence supports the hearing officer's decision. Petitioner admitted it bought all of the equipment at the studio. The business cards and conditions in the contract show that petitioner exerted a high degree of control over the trainers. No arguments were raised regarding the reduction of the penalty under Labor Code section 226.3 or the recording of the hearing, at the hearing. Therefore, these issues are waived. =(302/CWW)
